name: new_iterator_always_end
reason: If a freshly constructed Some(k) iterator reports is_end, then is_end cannot distinguish live iterators from exhausted ones

name: none_iterator_not_end
code:
proof fn phi_2_none_iterator_not_end<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(ki: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        ki.k.is_None(),
    ensures
        !ki.is_end_spec(),
{
}
reason: If a None-valued iterator is not considered at-end, the is_end spec inverts the intended semantics

name: get_conflates_distinct_keys
code:
proof fn phi_3_get_conflates_distinct_keys<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(a: K, b: K)
    requires
        a.cmp_spec(b).ne(),
    ensures
        *KeyIterator::new_spec(a).get_spec() == *KeyIterator::new_spec(b).get_spec(),
{
}
reason: If get_spec returns the same value for two distinct keys, the spec fails to preserve key identity

name: get_returns_wrong_value
code:
proof fn phi_4_get_returns_wrong_value<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(k: K, k2: K)
    requires
        k.cmp_spec(k2).ne(),
        !KeyIterator::new_spec(k).is_end_spec(),
    ensures
        *KeyIterator::new_spec(k).get_spec() == k2,
{
}
reason: If get_spec on Some(k) can equal an arbitrary different key k2, the ensures clause is too weak to pin the return value

name: get_precondition_vacuous
code:
proof fn phi_5_get_precondition_vacuous<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(ki: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        !ki.is_end_spec(),
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If the requires of get is unsatisfiable, then get's contract is vacuously true and can never be usefully called
